---
name: 3.04-reset-local-docker-pg
description: Stop and remove local PostgreSQL Docker container used for test data workflows
agent: agent
model: GPT-5.3-Codex (copilot)
---

# Reset Local Docker PostgreSQL Container

Prompt for a container name, then stop and remove the local PostgreSQL Docker container used for test data import workflows.

## Context
Default container from prompt 2.02 is `test-container`.

## Task
1. Prompt for the target container name (default: `test-container`).
2. Check whether the target container exists.
3. If running, stop the container.
4. Remove the container.
5. Verify the container no longer exists.

## Commands (Reference)
```pwsh
$containerName = Read-Host "Enter container name (default: test-container)"
if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($containerName)) { $containerName = "test-container" }

docker ps -a --filter "name=^/$containerName$" --format "{{.Names}} {{.Status}}"
docker stop $containerName
docker rm $containerName
docker ps -a --filter "name=^/$containerName$" --format "{{.Names}} {{.Status}}"
```

## Safety Requirements
- Do not delete unrelated containers.
- If the named container does not exist, report a no-op success.
- If the container is already stopped, proceed directly to removal.

## Success Criteria
- The selected container is stopped (if it was running).
- The selected container is removed from Docker.
- Final verification confirms no matching container exists.
